title: SwarmSort: Drone Delivery Zone Discovery
strategies: axis-split baseline: sort the drops by x-coordinate and cut into K equal-count contiguous bands; reproduces the evaluator's weak reference partition and ignores the true 2-D zone geometry
single-pass Lloyd K-means with a cheap deterministic init: recovers well-separated round zones but is init-sensitive and collapses on varied-spread, anisotropic, or non-convex zones

z-score standardised, K-means++ seeded, multi-restart K-means keeping the lowest-inertia partition: handles scale/anisotropy and escapes bad local optima, but as a centroidal method still cannot recover non-convex zones (crescents / rings)

shape-aware clustering: density- or connectivity-based methods (DBSCAN-style, single-linkage, spectral / graph-cut) or per-instance model selection that can bend around non-convex zones to push ARI toward the true partition on the hardest instances
